gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The refusal record emitted when a named partial accessor (`unwrap`/`expect`/`unwrap_err`)"] # [doc = " encounters the wrong variant. Carries the diagnostic as a message (C3 / spec §5/C3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The *mechanism* (abort vs escalate vs `std.diag` record) is co-designed with M-510/M-520"] # [doc = " (spec §7-Q3). This structure fixes the C3 obligation: the refusal is explicit and"] # [doc = " carries information. In this Rust-first implementation we panic with this record's"] # [doc = " message; the final form waits on `diag`/`recover` (FLAG Q3)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct RefusalRecord { # [doc = " The op name (`\"unwrap\"`, `\"expect\"`, or `\"unwrap_err\"`)."] pub op : & 'static str , # [doc = " A caller-supplied reason (empty string for `unwrap`)."] pub reason : String , # [doc = " The variant that was actually present (for diagnostics)."] pub actual_variant : & 'static str , }
gaps.reason: struct `RefusalRecord` named field(s) `op, reason, actual_variant` emitted positionally as `RefusalRecord(Bytes, Bytes, Bytes)` — Mycelium's `constructor` is positional-only (no record surface); product structure preserved, field names dropped (matches `lib/std/*.myc` hand-ports)

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; fn ok_42 () -> Result < i32 , & 'static str > { Ok (42) } fn err_oops () -> Result < i32 , & 'static str > { Err ("oops") } fn some_42 () -> Option < i32 > { Some (42) } fn none_i32 () -> Option < i32 > { None } # [doc = " `map` applies f to Ok; Err passes through unchanged (I1 / C1)."] # [doc = " Guard: a map that drops Err would pass Ok but this test catches it."] # [test] fn map_transforms_ok_err_passes_through () { assert_eq ! (map (ok_42 () , | x | x * 2) , Ok (84)) ; assert_eq ! (map (err_oops () , | x : i32 | x * 2) , Err ("oops")) ; } # [doc = " `map_err` applies f to Err; Ok passes through unchanged."] # [doc = " Guard: a map_err that drops Ok would pass Err but this test catches it."] # [test] fn map_err_transforms_err_ok_passes_through () { assert_eq ! (map_err (ok_42 () , | e : & str | e . len ()) , Ok (42)) ; assert_eq ! (map_err (err_oops () , | e | e . len ()) , Err (4)) ; } # [doc = " `and_then` propagates Err without calling f (never-silent: Err is not dropped)."] # [doc = " Guard: calling f on Err would yield a wrong result; this test uses a side effect"] # [doc = " to confirm f was not called."] # [test] fn and_then_short_circuits_on_err () { let called = core :: cell :: Cell :: new (false) ; let result = and_then (err_oops () , | _x : i32 | { called . set (true) ; Ok (0i32) }) ; assert_eq ! (result , Err ("oops") , "Err must propagate") ; assert ! (! called . get () , "f must not be called on Err (short-circuit)") ; } # [doc = " `and_then` applies f to Ok."] # [test] fn and_then_applies_f_on_ok () { assert_eq ! (and_then (ok_42 () , | x | Ok ::< _ , & str > (x + 1)) , Ok (43)) ; } # [doc = " `or_else` applies f to Err; Ok passes through."] # [test] fn or_else_applies_f_on_err_ok_passes_through () { assert_eq ! (or_else (err_oops () , | _e | Ok ::< i32 , i32 > (0)) , Ok (0)) ; assert_eq ! (or_else (ok_42 () , | _e : & str | Ok ::< _ , i32 > (0)) , Ok (42)) ; } # [doc = " `filter` converts Some to None when predicate is false (typed transition, not drop)."] # [doc = " Guard: treating None as a dropped Some would fail a downstream use — no sentinel."] # [test] fn filter_typed_transition_some_to_none () { assert_eq ! (filter (some_42 () , |& x | x > 100) , None) ; assert_eq ! (filter (some_42 () , |& x | x > 0) , Some (42)) ; assert_eq ! (filter (none_i32 () , |& x | x > 0) , None) ; } # [doc = " `inspect` peeks Ok without changing the sum."] # [test] fn inspect_peeks_ok_sum_unchanged () { let mut seen = None ; let r = inspect (ok_42 () , | & x | seen = Some (x)) ; assert_eq ! (r , Ok (42)) ; assert_eq ! (seen , Some (42)) ; } # [doc = " `inspect_err` peeks Err without changing propagation."] # [test] fn inspect_err_peeks_err_propagation_unchanged () { let mut seen = None ; let r = inspect_err (err_oops () , | & e | seen = Some (e)) ; assert_eq ! (r , Err ("oops")) ; assert_eq ! (seen , Some ("oops")) ; } # [doc = " `inspect` on Err does NOT call f (Err propagates unchanged)."] # [test] fn inspect_on_err_does_not_call_f () { let called = core :: cell :: Cell :: new (false) ; let r = inspect (err_oops () , | _ | called . set (true)) ; assert_eq ! (r , Err ("oops")) ; assert ! (! called . get ()) ; } # [doc = " `inspect_err` on Ok does NOT call f (Ok propagates unchanged)."] # [test] fn inspect_err_on_ok_does_not_call_f () { let called = core :: cell :: Cell :: new (false) ; let r = inspect_err (ok_42 () , | _ : & & str | called . set (true)) ; assert_eq ! (r , Ok (42)) ; assert ! (! called . get ()) ; } # [doc = " `ok_or` names None as Err explicitly (never-silent)."] # [test] fn ok_or_names_none_as_err () { assert_eq ! (ok_or (some_42 () , "missing") , Ok (42)) ; assert_eq ! (ok_or (none_i32 () , "missing") , Err ("missing")) ; } # [doc = " `ok_or_else` names None as Err with a lazy error."] # [test] fn ok_or_else_lazy_error_for_none () { let called = core :: cell :: Cell :: new (false) ; let r = ok_or_else (some_42 () , | | { called . set (true) ; "missing" }) ; assert_eq ! (r , Ok (42)) ; assert ! (! called . get () , "f must not be called for Some") ; let r2 = ok_or_else (none_i32 () , | | "lazily-computed") ; assert_eq ! (r2 , Err ("lazily-computed")) ; } # [doc = " `ok` converts Ok(t) to Some(t) and Err to None."] # [doc = " This is the one flagged lossy conversion (spec §7-Q2). Test confirms the conversion"] # [doc = " is explicit — the caller calls `ok` intentionally; there is no silent swallow."] # [test] fn ok_lossy_conversion_explicit () { assert_eq ! (ok (ok_42 ()) , Some (42)) ; assert_eq ! (ok (err_oops ()) , None) ; } # [doc = " `transpose` lifts Err from inside Option to outside."] # [test] fn transpose_propagates_err () { let inner_err : Option < Result < i32 , & str > > = Some (Err ("inner")) ; assert_eq ! (transpose (inner_err) , Err ("inner")) ; assert_eq ! (transpose (Some (Ok ::< i32 , & str > (1))) , Ok (Some (1))) ; assert_eq ! (transpose (None ::< Result < i32 , & str >>) , Ok (None)) ; } # [doc = " `flatten` propagates the inner Err."] # [test] fn flatten_propagates_inner_err () { let inner : Result < Result < i32 , & str > , & str > = Ok (Err ("inner")) ; assert_eq ! (flatten (inner) , Err ("inner")) ; assert_eq ! (flatten (Ok (Ok ::< i32 , & str > (7))) , Ok (7)) ; assert_eq ! (flatten (Err ::< Result < i32 , & str >, & str > ("outer")) , Err ("outer")) ; } # [doc = " `zip` short-circuits to None when either is None."] # [test] fn zip_short_circuits_on_none () { assert_eq ! (zip (some_42 () , Some ("hi")) , Some ((42 , "hi"))) ; assert_eq ! (zip (none_i32 () , Some ("hi")) , None) ; assert_eq ! (zip (some_42 () , None ::<& str >) , None) ; } # [doc = " `unwrap_or` recovers Err with an explicitly supplied default; records Declared tag."] # [test] fn unwrap_or_recovers_with_declared_tag () { let (val , record) = unwrap_or (ok_42 () , 0) ; assert_eq ! (val , 42 , "Ok value must be returned") ; assert_eq ! (record . guarantee_tag , "Declared") ; let (val2 , record2) = unwrap_or (err_oops () , 99) ; assert_eq ! (val2 , 99 , "default must be returned for Err") ; assert_eq ! (record2 . guarantee_tag , "Declared") ; assert_eq ! (record2 . op , "unwrap_or") ; } # [doc = " `unwrap_or_else` recovers Err with a computed default; records Declared tag."] # [test] fn unwrap_or_else_recovers_with_declared_tag () { let (val , record) = unwrap_or_else (err_oops () , | _e | 99) ; assert_eq ! (val , 99) ; assert_eq ! (record . guarantee_tag , "Declared") ; assert_eq ! (record . op , "unwrap_or_else") ; } # [doc = " The `Declared` tag is never `Exact` — downgrade is the rule (VR-5)."] # [doc = " Guard: changing guarantee_tag to \"Exact\" in the record makes this fail."] # [test] fn unwrap_or_tag_is_not_exact () { let (_ , record) = unwrap_or (err_oops () , 0) ; assert_ne ! (record . guarantee_tag , "Exact" , "unwrap_or must not claim Exact — the default is Declared (VR-5/I2)") ; } # [doc = " `unwrap` on Ok returns the value."] # [test] fn unwrap_on_ok_returns_value () { assert_eq ! (unwrap (ok_42 ()) , 42) ; } # [doc = " `unwrap` on Err panics (refuses loudly — never a silent default)."] # [doc = " Guard: returning a sentinel/default instead of panicking makes this fail."] # [test] # [should_panic (expected = "unwrap")] fn unwrap_on_err_refuses_loudly () { unwrap (err_oops ()) ; } # [doc = " `expect` on Ok returns the value."] # [test] fn expect_on_ok_returns_value () { assert_eq ! (expect (ok_42 () , "must be ok") , 42) ; } # [doc = " `expect` on Err panics with the caller-supplied message."] # [doc = " Guard: a silent default would not panic with the caller message."] # [test] # [should_panic (expected = "caller-reason")] fn expect_on_err_refuses_loudly_with_msg () { expect (err_oops () , "caller-reason") ; } # [doc = " `unwrap_err` on Err returns the error."] # [test] fn unwrap_err_on_err_returns_error () { assert_eq ! (unwrap_err (err_oops ()) , "oops") ; } # [doc = " `unwrap_err` on Ok panics (refuses loudly — symmetric partial)."] # [doc = " Guard: returning a sentinel instead of panicking makes this fail."] # [test] # [should_panic (expected = "unwrap_err")] fn unwrap_err_on_ok_refuses_loudly () { unwrap_err (ok_42 ()) ; } # [doc = " Property: `map` is the identity on `Err` — for all error shapes, Err passes through."] # [doc = " This is the per-op \"never-silent\" property bound for `map` (spec §4)."] # [test] fn property_map_never_drops_err () { for & e in & ["a" , "b" , "long error" , "" , "oops"] { let r : Result < i32 , & str > = Err (e) ; let out = map (r , | x | x * 100) ; assert_eq ! (out , Err (e) , "map must preserve Err: {e:?}") ; } } # [doc = " Property: `map_err` is the identity on `Ok` — for all ok values, Ok passes through."] # [test] fn property_map_err_never_drops_ok () { for & v in & [0i32 , 1 , - 1 , i32 :: MAX , i32 :: MIN] { let r : Result < i32 , & str > = Ok (v) ; let out = map_err (r , | e : & str | e . len ()) ; assert_eq ! (out , Ok (v) , "map_err must preserve Ok: {v}") ; } } # [doc = " Property: `and_then` propagates Err for all error values (never-silent bound)."] # [test] fn property_and_then_propagates_all_errs () { for & e in & ["e1" , "e2" , "error3"] { let r : Result < i32 , & str > = Err (e) ; let out = and_then (r , | _ | Ok :: < i32 , & str > (999)) ; assert_eq ! (out , Err (e) , "and_then must propagate Err: {e:?}") ; } } # [doc = " Property: `or_else` passes through Ok for all ok values."] # [test] fn property_or_else_passes_through_ok () { for & v in & [0i32 , 42 , - 99] { let r : Result < i32 , & str > = Ok (v) ; let out = or_else (r , | _ | Ok :: < _ , i32 > (0)) ; assert_eq ! (out , Ok (v) , "or_else must pass through Ok: {v}") ; } } # [doc = " Property: `filter` typed transition — `filter(None, _)` is always None."] # [test] fn property_filter_none_is_always_none () { for pred_result in & [true , false] { let out = filter (none_i32 () , | _ | * pred_result) ; assert_eq ! (out , None , "filter(None, _) must be None") ; } } # [doc = " Property: `ok_or` never returns Ok for None (naming the absence explicitly)."] # [test] fn property_ok_or_names_none_as_err () { for & e in & ["e1" , "e2" , "missing"] { let out : Result < i32 , & str > = ok_or (none_i32 () , e) ; assert ! (out . is_err () , "ok_or(None, e) must be Err for any e") ; assert_eq ! (out . unwrap_err () , e) ; } } # [doc = " Property: `unwrap_or` result for Err is exactly the supplied default (Declared)."] # [test] fn property_unwrap_or_exact_default_for_err () { for & default in & [0i32 , 1 , 99 , - 1 , i32 :: MIN] { let r : Result < i32 , & str > = Err ("e") ; let (val , record) = unwrap_or (r , default) ; assert_eq ! (val , default , "unwrap_or must return exactly the default for Err") ; assert_eq ! (record . guarantee_tag , "Declared" , "tag must be Declared (VR-5)") ; } } # [doc = " Property: `zip` always yields None when either input is None."] # [test] fn property_zip_none_absorbs () { for a in [some_42 () , none_i32 ()] { let out = zip (a , None :: < i32 >) ; assert_eq ! (out , None , "zip with None must be None") ; } } # [doc = " Property: `transpose` preserves the Err inside Some."] # [test] fn property_transpose_preserves_inner_err () { for & e in & ["e1" , "e2" , "err"] { let inner : Option < Result < i32 , & str > > = Some (Err (e)) ; assert_eq ! (transpose (inner) , Err (e)) ; } } # [doc = " Property: `flatten` propagates both inner and outer Errs."] # [test] fn property_flatten_propagates_all_errs () { let errs = ["outer" , "inner"] ; let inner : Result < Result < i32 , & str > , & str > = Ok (Err (errs [1])) ; assert_eq ! (flatten (inner) , Err ("inner")) ; let outer : Result < Result < i32 , & str > , & str > = Err (errs [0]) ; assert_eq ! (flatten (outer) , Err ("outer")) ; } # [doc = " SubstitutionRecord carries the honest `Declared` tag."] # [test] fn substitution_record_carries_declared () { let r = SubstitutionRecord { op : "unwrap_or" , guarantee_tag : "Declared" , } ; assert_eq ! (r . guarantee_tag , "Declared") ; } # [doc = " RefusalRecord Display includes op name (C3 diagnostic)."] # [test] fn refusal_record_display_includes_op () { let r = RefusalRecord { op : "expect" , reason : "should have been ok" . to_owned () , actual_variant : "Err" , } ; let msg = r . to_string () ; assert ! (msg . contains ("expect") , "display must include op name") ; assert ! (msg . contains ("should have been ok") , "display must include reason") ; assert ! (msg . contains ("Err") , "display must include actual variant") ; } }
gaps.reason: #[cfg(test)] item — out of scope for this PoC's transpilation surface (excluded from the expressible-fraction denominator, but recorded, never silently skipped)
